name: ABF substrates (high-layer-count)
severityNote: Easing from '22-'24 critical, AI SKUs still constrained
description: Ajinomoto Build-up Film substrates with 14–20+ layer counts are the package-level interposer between BGA and PCB for every AI accelerator, CPU, and FPGA. The high-layer-count, large-body SKUs used by Rubin and MI400-class parts remain capacity-constrained even as mainstream substrate supply has eased.
stackPosition: ABF film (Ajinomoto monopoly) → laminated multi-layer build-up at Ibiden/Unimicron/Shinko → drill, plate, pattern, repeat for 14–22 layers → outer-layer routing → singulation → ship to TSMC for CoWoS attach OR direct to BGA package. The AI-class SKUs (large body, ultra-high layer count) come from a small subset of lines at the top-3 makers; mainstream PC/server substrates do not consume the same equipment.

supplyStructure.name: Shinko Electric
supplyStructure.note: Taken private by JIC consortium (2024); 6967.T delisted

indicators.name: Ibiden IC package substrate revenue and AI-SKU mix commentary
Ajinomoto electronic materials division ABF film volume
Shinko / JIC takeover close + capex re-direction

companies.name: Ibiden
companies.note: Highest AI-SKU concentration; Ogaki capacity is the constraint

companies.name: Unimicron
companies.note: Taiwan-side leader; tighter coupling to TSMC packaging

companies.name: Shinko Electric
companies.note: Taken private by JIC consortium (2024); capex direction under new ownership is the key watch item

companies.name: Ajinomoto
companies.note: ABF film monopoly; pricing power on each new node

insight: The 2022 ABF shortage created a forest of new capacity that's now coming online into a softer mainstream market — but the AI-SKU sub-market behaves like a separate good. Ibiden's incremental capacity is structurally captured by NVIDIA/AMD demand, while mainstream PC/server substrate pricing decays. The investable wedge is Ibiden specifically, not the basket: it is the only top-3 maker whose AI-SKU mix is growing into existing line capacity rather than via greenfield. The Shinko transition is the wild card — capex re-direction by the new owner could either tighten or loosen the niche.

debates.question: Does Ibiden's AI-SKU revenue mix exceed 35% by end of 2027?
debates.currentBelief: Likely on current trajectory — Ogaki capacity allocation skews more AI each quarter

debates.question: Does mainstream ABF substrate ASPs roll over by 2H 2026 as new capacity ramps?
debates.currentBelief: Yes for PC/standard server; AI-class SKUs remain firm
